page: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge is a graphics-intensive instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor with an x86_64 architecture, providing 32 dedicated vCPUs (16 physical cores with 2 threads per core) running at 3.3 GHz. The system includes 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM, and 900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 25 Gbps.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU-bound and GPU-accelerated workloads. The instance exhibits poor single-core CPU performance, placing in the bottom 10% on stress-ng tests, while multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth benchmarks fall into the average category. However, the integrated NVIDIA A10G GPU delivers top-tier performance (top 10%) for large language model (LLM) inference, specifically for prompt processing and text generation using small (135M) and medium (7B) models. Prompt processing for large (70B) models is also strong, ranking in the top 25%.

From a qualitative cost-efficiency perspective, the g5.8xlarge offers a balanced resource density for workloads that can offload computation to the GPU, offsetting the lower single-core CPU efficiency. The main tradeoff lies in the contrast between its average general-purpose compute capabilities and its highly efficient parallel processing performance. This hardware profile is suited for LLM inference, graphics-intensive applications, machine learning workloads, and database operations that leverage GPU acceleration and local NVMe storage.
description: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge is an x86_64 graphics-intensive instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs from an AMD EPYC 7R32 processor running at 3.3 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of DDR4 memory. It includes a single NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB of VRAM and 900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, supported by a 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. While single-core CPU benchmarks rank in the bottom 10%, the instance delivers top-tier performance in the top 10% for small and medium LLM inference tasks. Multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth benchmarks remain average. Qualitatively, the instance provides high resource density for GPU-dependent tasks, making it a viable option for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database operations that require local NVMe storage and hardware acceleration rather than raw single-threaded CPU performance.
og_description: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge is an x86_64 instance with 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, a 22 GB NVIDIA A10G GPU, and 900 GB NVMe SSD storage, delivering top-tier performance for LLM inference.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services g5.8xlarge features 32 vCPUs, 128 GB RAM, an NVIDIA A10G GPU, and 900 GB NVMe storage for GPU-accelerated workloads.
tagline: An x86_64 GPU-accelerated instance featuring an NVIDIA A10G GPU, local NVMe storage, and top-tier performance for LLM inference workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R32 CPU with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB DDR4 RAM
NVIDIA Ampere A10G GPU with 22 GB VRAM for hardware acceleration
900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage and 25 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Top-tier performance in the top 10% for small and medium LLM inference
Suited for machine learning inference, graphics rendering, and database operations
